tried_strategies.goal: 3 hook additions: synonym map + temporal timeline + G2a importers
G1 과적합 검증 + 일반화 수정 (word-boundary verbs + deep grep _is_decision() 제거)
G1 일반화 수치 완성 — CTX 59-query + Flask + Requests 교차 검증 완전 비교표
G1 Recall@7 상한선 분석 + 0-7d 개선 — miss pattern 분류, multi-keyword 도입

known_facts: Temporal timeline mode: _TEMPORAL_QUERY_RE detects history/progression/timeline queries → [DECISION TIMELINE] header + date-prefixed oldest-first output
G2a 1-hop import discovery: git grep reverse importers on G2-found Python files; cost <2s per file
Remaining 7/15 misses = live-inf iter commits filtered by _OMC_ITER_RE (structural noise, intended behavior)
New practical ceiling: ~0.75-0.80 (if live-inf iter filtering relaxed: ~0.88)
G1 overfitting root cause: _is_decision() biased to CTX-domain keywords (benchmark/eval/iter/CONVERGED)
Flask commits fail _is_decision() because they use descriptive language — fixed by Pass0 gate removal
_DECISION_VERBS_RE with word-boundary regex: \badd\b does NOT match 'address' — safe to use
Pass 0 deep grep: removing _is_decision() gate allows surfacing general-project commits via keyword grep
Pass0 miss root cause: long_kws ≥4-char filter missed 'git-memory' (hyphen-split), '2040' (numeric), 'CTX' (3-char)
Fix: extract_keywords() now emits compound terms + numeric tokens; Pass0 uses up to 5 grep_kws, max-count=10
Remaining 17/45 0-7d misses: ~8 live-inf iter N/∞ (topic-dedup collapse), ~5 near-duplicate commits (same topic cluster), ~4 keyword-poor
Practical ceiling for keyword-based retrieval: ~0.65-0.70 on this benchmark (structural dedup limit)
BM25 cross-domain: zero-score → stable sort → no harmful reordering for unrelated queries

uncertain.question: Can semantic reranking push 0-7d recall above 0.70 for structural miss cases?
uncertain.status: unmeasured — would require vec-daemon to be available + live testing

uncertain.question: Is the live-inf iter topic-dedup collapse avoidable with a different dedup strategy?
uncertain.status: structural limit — all iter commits touch .omc/ files, same topic key
